index_status.note: Both indexes were refreshed/rebuilt for this audit; status remains stale because tracked generated outputs changed after index content generation.

findings.title: Helper/script/config-schema catalog is stale relative to the repo it is supposed to route
findings.impact: Agents following AGENTS/SKILL helper routing can miss active validators/builders and either duplicate behavior, skip necessary validators, or treat current scripts as neither active nor archived.

findings.title: Repo index and script-import index refreshes leave their own status stale/dirty
findings.impact: Normal index-refresh workflows can become repeat-rebuild loops or false blockers because generated/tracked summary, receipt, manifest, checkpoint, and sqlite outputs are treated as stale immediately after writing.

findings.title: Existing config/schema/contract validators pass while covering only a small slice of the estate
findings.impact: A passing preflight currently does not prove the 223 config files, 167 schemas, or 197 contracts are globally cataloged, bound, or validated.

findings.title: Bad-pattern scanners are not clean enough to serve as decisive audit gates
findings.impact: The scanner reports real review targets mixed with false positives: secret hits are validator string literals, pct_change hits include real pandas calls and custom helper names, and the test-write hit is a fixture-style temp repo test.

findings.title: Archived foundation references still leak into source/config/test/generated audit surfaces
findings.impact: The guard reports 37 violations, including an active src reference to a legacy proof path and unallowlisted foundation path references in config/test/manifests. This weakens the current-authority boundary.

findings.title: Several exact path refs used by configs/checkpoints do not resolve in the active repo index
findings.impact: Agents and validators using exact path lookup can treat historical/proposal/archive-internal refs as missing runtime surfaces unless these are normalized to archive-member metadata or downgraded to evidence-only text.

findings.title: Many scripts accept --summary-only while still containing write paths; several use --dry-run instead of --write
findings.impact: Some are intentional report generators, but the CLI vocabulary is inconsistent. It is easy to assume --summary-only means read-only when a script actually writes tmp reports or run summaries.

findings.title: ValueInvesting is gone from active source registry surfaces, but historical docs still contain many references
findings.impact: This is not an active provider-lane problem, but broad text search still surfaces retired provider references from historical bundles and may confuse future source audits.
